text: The Daniels VDOT calculator significantly underestimates laboratory-measured VO2max in both collegiate athletes and recreational runners when based on 5km time trial performance.
The VDOT underestimation of VO2max is significantly greater in recreational runners than in collegiate athletes, with a large effect size.
Daniels' recommended interval pace is significantly slower than actual pace at VO2max in recreational runners, suggesting it may be suboptimal for improving VO2max in this population.
Daniels' recommended interval pace closely matches actual pace at VO2max in collegiate athletes, making it appropriate for interval training in trained runners.
Daniels' recommended threshold pace is faster than laboratory-measured lactate threshold pace in collegiate athletes but not significantly different in recreational runners.
Daniels' threshold pace can be confidently used for threshold training regardless of ability level, as it approximates or slightly exceeds lactate threshold pace.
Recreational runners may need to run interval sessions faster than Daniels' recommended interval pace to optimally stimulate VO2max improvements.
